source_text: “Much obliged,” he said, briefly, to Ikey. “The lazy Irish loafer! My own room’s just above Rosy’s. I’ll just go up there myself after supper and load the shot-gun and wait. If he comes in my back yard he’ll go away in a ambulance instead of a bridal chaise.”
raw_output: “Much obliged,” he said, briefly, to Ikey. “The lazy Irish loafer! My own room’s right above Rosy’s. I’ll just go up there myself after supper and load the shot-gun and wait. If he comes into my back yard, he’ll go away in an ambulance instead of a bridal chaise.”
rewrite_text: “Much obliged,” he said, briefly, to Ikey. “The lazy Irish loafer! My own room’s right above Rosy’s. I’ll just go up there myself after supper and load the shot-gun and wait. If he comes into my back yard, he’ll go away in an ambulance instead of a bridal chaise.”

source_text: “Let him speak! The king! The king! his humble subjects hunger and thirst for words of wisdom out of the mouth of their master his Serene and Sacred Raggedness!”
raw_output: “Let him speak! The king! The king! His humble subjects hunger and thirst for words of wisdom from the mouth of their master, his Serene and Sacred Raggedness!”
rewrite_text: “Let him speak! The king! The king! His humble subjects hunger and thirst for words of wisdom from the mouth of their master, his Serene and Sacred Raggedness!”

source_text: “What the devil made you stand lingering there, in the wet?” said Monks, turning round, and addressing Bumble, after he had bolted the door behind them.
raw_output: “What the devil made you stand there lingering in the wet?” said Monks, turning about, and addressing Bumble, after he had bolted the door behind them.
rewrite_text: “What the devil made you stand there lingering in the wet?” said Monks, turning about, and addressing Bumble, after he had bolted the door behind them.

source_text: where z x and z y are latent variables for question and answer respectively, and the p ψ (z x |c) and p ψ (z y |z x , c) are their conditional priors following an isotropic Gaussian distribution and a categorical distribution ( Figure 1 -(a)). We decompose the latent space of question and answer, since the answer is always a finite span of context c, which can be modeled well by a categorical distribution, while a continuous latent space is a more appropriate choice for question since there could be unlimited valid questions from a single context. Moreover, we design the bi-directional dependency flow of joint distribution for QA. By leveraging hierarchical structure, we enforce the answer latent variables to be dependent on the question latent variables in p ψ (z y |z x , c) and achieve the reverse dependency by sampling question
raw_output: where z x and z y denote the latent variables for the question and answer, respectively, and p ψ (z x |c) and p ψ (z y |z x , c) denote their conditional priors, following an isotropic Gaussian distribution and a categorical distribution, respectively (Figure 1-(a)). We separate the latent spaces of the question and answer because the answer is always a finite span of context c, which can be effectively represented by a categorical distribution, whereas a continuous latent space is a more suitable choice for the question, since a single context may admit an unlimited number of valid questions. In addition, we construct a bidirectional dependency flow in the joint QA distribution. By exploiting the hierarchical structure, we constrain the answer latent variables to depend on the question latent variables in p ψ (z y |z x , c) and obtain the reverse dependency by sampling question
